title: Dungeon Degenerates - Part One - Introduction, the story so far, and some set up
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YA85ddRTnus

Daniel (@dungeondive) welcomes us to what's already shaping up as his favorite game—Dungeon Degenerates: Hand of Doom. After running through the brutal story so far, we meet his two protagonists: a human hunter rogue named the Vermin Hunter and a bloodsport brawler fighter. These two jailbird degenerates have just escaped the dank fortress city of Brutal Burg with a mysterious severed head in tow—a soothsayer's skull they eventually sold off for cash rather than deliver to either the corrupt jailer or the mystical stone circle warlocks.

Daniel digs into the game's staggering art direction—a punk-metal aesthetic inspired by underground comix legends. The bold, audacious style immediately sets Dungeon Degenerates apart from sterile fantasy offerings. Every card drips with grimy personality, from the loot to the grotesque monster designs. It's dangerous, uncompromising art that won't appeal to everyone, but those who grew up on metal bands and outsider art recognize this as something special.

The campaign system is where Dungeon Degenerates truly shines. With 20 different missions, each packed with branching paths and player agency, the game rewards bold decisions and punishes safe ones. Daniel's already living proof—his party escaped jail, traveled through haunted lands, made deals with aristocrats, and survived the rotten swamp. Now they're homeless and broke, ready to establish a base in this grimy world gone mad.

----

**What draws you to the grittier side of tabletop gaming—would you play a campaign as grimy and unpolished as Dungeon Degenerates?**
